name: data-analysis
description: Consultas de analytics, agregação de dados, métricas de dashboard e exportação de relatórios

query_patterns.total_generations: SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM generations WHERE user_id = ?
query_patterns.generations_by_tool: SELECT t.name, COUNT(*) as count FROM generations g JOIN tools t ON g.tool_id = t.id WHERE g.user_id = ? GROUP BY t.name ORDER BY count DESC
query_patterns.generations_by_period: SELECT DATE(created_at) as date, COUNT(*) as count FROM generations WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at >= date('now', '-30 days') GROUP BY DATE(created_at) ORDER BY date
query_patterns.project_count: SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM projects WHERE user_id = ?
query_patterns.client_count: SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM clients WHERE user_id = ?
query_patterns.pipeline_value: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(value), 0) as total FROM opportunities WHERE user_id = ? AND stage NOT IN ('closed_lost', 'archived')
query_patterns.recent_activity: SELECT 'generation' as type, created_at, tool_id as ref FROM generations WHERE user_id = ? UNION ALL SELECT 'project' as type, updated_at, name FROM projects WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10

queries.total_users: SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM users
queries.users_by_plan: SELECT COALESCE(s.plan_id, 'free') as plan, COUNT(*) as count FROM users u LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON u.id = s.user_id GROUP BY plan
queries.users_by_role: SELECT role, COUNT(*) as count FROM users GROUP BY role
queries.active_today: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as count FROM generations WHERE DATE(created_at) = DATE('now')
queries.total_revenue: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(p.price), 0) as total FROM subscriptions s JOIN plans p ON s.plan_id = p.id WHERE s.status = 'active'
queries.top_tools: SELECT t.name, COUNT(*) as count FROM generations g JOIN tools t ON g.tool_id = t.id GROUP BY t.id ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 5
queries.avg_daily_generations: SELECT AVG(daily) as avg FROM (SELECT DATE(created_at) as day, COUNT(*) as daily FROM generations GROUP BY day)

export_module.csv_generation: Usar cabeçalhos manuais + join: const csv = [headers.join(','), ...rows.map(r => fields.map(f => r[f]).join(','))].join('\n'); res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/csv')

endpoints.client: GET /api/export/client/:id — dados do cliente + oportunidades + interações
endpoints.pipeline: GET /api/export/pipeline — todas oportunidades com nome do cliente

frontend_dashboard.visualization: Usar CSS Grid para cards de métricas, barras com divs width percentual (sem biblioteca gráfica pesada)
frontend_dashboard.metrics_cards: Cada métrica: label, valor, ícone, tendência (up/down), cor de destaque
frontend_dashboard.period_selector: Filtro de período: 7d, 30d, 90d, 12m — passar como query param para o back

admin_dashboard.data_fetch: Chamar endpoints admin para métricas globais
admin_dashboard.sections: Usuários totais, Usuários ativos hoje, Receita mensal, Top ferramentas, Distribuição de planos, Distribuição de roles

conventions.user_scoping: Todas queries de analytics são scoped por user_id (multi-tenancy)
conventions.date_handling: Usar DATE() do SQLite para agrupar por dia. evitar funções JavaScript para datas no banco
conventions.null_handling: Sempre COALESCE para valores numéricos (SUM, COUNT) para evitar null no response
conventions.pagination: Listas grandes usar LIMIT + OFFSET com query params ?page=1&limit=50
conventions.caching: Dados de analytics com TTL de 5 minutos — armazenar em memória ou revalidar a cada fetch
